notes.question: Playwright Locators and UI commands
notes.answer:
// Click
await page.locator('button#submit').click();

// Right Click
await page.locator('button#submit').click({ button: 'right' });

// Mouse Over (Hover)
await page.locator('.menu-item').hover();

// For a variable
const text = await page.locator('.welcome-msg').innerText();

// For an assertion (Recommended)
await expect(page.locator('.welcome-msg')).toHaveText('Welcome, User');

// Select by value, label, or index
await page.locator('select#country').selectOption('USA'); // Value
await page.locator('select#country').selectOption({ label: 'India' }); // Label

// Select the iframe first, then the element inside
const loginFrame = page.frameLocator('#login-iframe');
await loginFrame.locator('#username').fill('admin');

// Start waiting for the popup before clicking
const popupPromise = page.waitForEvent('popup');
await page.locator('#open-window-btn').click();

// Use the new page object
const newTab = await popupPromise;
await newTab.waitForLoadState();
console.log(await newTab.title());
await newTab.locator('#close').click();

await page.locator('selector').fill('text')
await page.locator('selector').check()
await page.locator('selector').dblclick()
await page.locator(src).dragTo(page.locator(dest))

notes.question: Session storage/session authentication
notes.answer:
To insert only one record in session storage.

test('@SP Place the order', async ({ page }) => {
  page.addInitScript(value => {

    window.localStorage.setItem('token', value);
  }, response.token);

To save session strorage.

test.beforeAll(async ({ browser }) => {
    const context = await browser.newContext();
    const page = await context.newPage();
    await page.goto("https://rahulshettyacademy.com/client");
    await page.locator("[value='Login']").click();
    await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');
    await context.storageState({ path: 'state.json' });
    webContext = await browser.newContext({ storageState: 'state.json' }); }

notes.question: Custom report generation
notes.answer:
By implementing the Reporter interface methods we can achieve it

Common methods and implementations

onBegin(config, suite)

onTestBegin(test, result)

onStepBegin(test, result, step)

onStepEnd(test, result, step)

onTestEnd(test, result)

onEnd(result)

onStdOut(chunk, test, result)

onError(error)

class MyCustomReporter {
  onBegin(config, suite) {
    console.log(`Starting the run with ${suite.allTests().length} tests`);
  }

  onTestBegin(test) {
    console.log(`>> Running test: ${test.title}`);
  }

  onTestEnd(test, result) {
    console.log(`Finished test ${test.title}: ${result.status.toUpperCase()}`);
    if (result.status === 'failed') {
      console.log(`Error: ${result.error.message}`);
    }
  }

  async onEnd(result) {
    console.log(`Overall Run Result: ${result.status}`);
    // Here you could send data to an API or DB
  }
}

module.exports = MyCustomReporter;

notes.question: How to handle SSL Certification and proxy setup
notes.answer:
Proxy setup:

In config file you can update the proxy setup globaly, or in brower level u can

// playwright.config.js
module.exports = {
  use: {
    proxy: {
      server: 'http://myproxy.com:3128',
      username: 'myUser', // Optional for authenticated proxies
      password: 'myPassword',
      bypass: 'localhost' // List of hosts that should not use the proxy
    },
  },
};

SSL Certificates:

In config u can mention to ignore Ceritificate errors

module.exports = {
  use: {
    ignoreHTTPSErrors: true,
  },
};

Parallel execution can be achevied by increasing workers count in Playwright.config.json file.

Same for retries also, can be config as Retries fields

const { defineConfig, devices } = require ('@playwright/test');


module.exports = defineConfig({
  testDir: './tests',

  /* Reporter to use. See https://playwright.dev/docs/test-reporters */
  reporter: 'html',
  retries :1,
  workers: 3,

notes.question: Hooks and/vs fixtures
notes.answer:
For complex setups (like "logging in"), Playwright experts often prefer using Custom Fixtures over hooks because fixtures are more reusable and handle teardown automatically.


Hooks used to control the execution flow.

test.beforeAll(async()=> {})

test.beforeEach(async()=> {})

test('name',async({page})=> {})

test.afterAll(async()=> {})

test.afterEach(async()=> {})

const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');

test.describe('User Profile Tests', () => {

  // Runs once before any tests start
  test.beforeAll(async () => {
    console.log('Setup: Starting Test Suite...');
  });

  // Runs before each individual test
  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('https://example.com');
    await page.locator('#username').fill('testuser');
    await page.locator('#password').fill('password123');
    await page.click('#login-button');
  });

  test('should display profile name', async ({ page }) => {
    await expect(page.locator('.profile-name')).toHaveText('testuser');
  });

  test('should allow password change', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.click('.settings');
    // ... test logic
  });

  // Runs after each individual test
  test.afterEach(async ({ page }) => {
    console.log('Finished a test. Cleaning up session...');
  });

  // Runs once after all tests are done
  test.afterAll(async () => {
    console.log('Teardown: All tests completed.');
  });
});

notes.question: DB testing
notes.answer:
Install DB libraries and have the support utils

// dbUtils.js
const mysql = require('mysql2/promise');

async function query(sql, params) {
  const connection = await mysql.createConnection({
    host: 'localhost',
    user: 'root',
    password: 'password',
    database: 'test_db'
  });
  
  const [results] = await connection.execute(sql, params);
  await connection.end();
  return results;
}

module.exports = { query };

notes.question: Sync and Async
notes.answer:
Synchronous - Line by line execution

Async - all lines will execute

By nature js is sync, but all playwright lib are in async, so we use await to handle it

notes.question: Java DSA
How to reduce execution time
notes.answer:
1. remove hard waits and give dynamic waits

  2. Increase thread count

  3. Reprioritise the test cases

  4. use API mocking abort to reduce browser loading time

notes.question: How to handle Overlaping UI Elements
notes.answer:
We can handle in 4 ways.

  1. Use force: true

If an element is technically in the DOM but covered by a transparent overlay or a fading loading spinner, you can bypass the "actionability" checks.

// This clicks the element even if it's covered
await page.locator('button#submit').click({ force: true });

  1. Wait for the Overlay to Disappear (Recommended)

// Wait for the spinner to go away first
await page.locator('.loading-spinner').waitFor({ state: 'hidden' });
await page.locator('button#submit').click();

  1. Use Dispatch Events (JavaScript Click)

// Dispatches a 'click' event directly to the DOM element
await page.locator('button#submit').dispatchEvent('click');

  1. Coordinate-Based Click

// Clicks at 5px from the top and 5px from the left of the element
await page.locator('button#submit').click({ position: { x: 5, y: 5 } });

notes.question: What is CORS?
notes.answer:
Cross Origin resource sharing - > it will restrict eh sharing if its not whitelisted.

to handle it in Playwright:

  1. in config file mention it liek below

use: {
  launchOptions: {
    args: ['--disable-web-security'] // Bypasses CORS and Same-Origin policy
  }
}

  1. Use route to mock the response

await page.route('**/api/**', async route => {
  const response = await route.fetch();
  await route.fulfill({
    response,
    headers: {
      ...response.headers(),
      'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*', // Force allow all origins
    }
  });
});

notes.question: Explain Project Architechture
notes.answer:
Tests -> to keep all my test files in spec.js format

POM -> to keep all my page object files

Fixtures -> to keep my custom fixture

Custom Report -> reporting structure for custom reports by implemntion reported interface

My repots -> to keep all reports

Utils -> to keep all helper functions and API Files

package.json -> for dependency maintance and script aliasing

Playwright.config.json -> to keep browser config (dir,thread,timeouts,retries)(under use headless,video,screenshots,traces)(under project browser details will be maintaing)

notes.question: Types of Wait statements
notes.answer:
Auto Wait:

by using the await, it will wait until the line to be actionalble.

await page.locator('#submit').click();

Web First Assertion:

By using expect i can wait until the element state.

await expect(page.locator('.status')).toHaveText('Submitted');

Network Waiting:

await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');

await page.waitForFunction(() => window.innerWidth > 1000);

Hard Waiting:

await page.waitForTimeout(3000);

notes.question: Custom Fixture creation for Utils and POM
notes.answer:
Fixture can be created by expanding the base test as like below.

By using this we can remove PO manager dependencies.

const base = require('@playwright/test');
const {LoginPage} = require ('../PageObjects/LoginPage');
const {xmlHelper} = require('../Utils/XMLParser/xmlHelper');

module.exports = base.test.extend({
    POManger: async ({page},use)=>{
        const LoginPO = new LoginPage(page)
        await use(LoginPO);
    },
    xmlHelpers: async ({},use)=>{
        const xmlHelpers = new xmlHelper();
        await use(xmlHelpers);
    }
})

Here ,

  1. Setup - will be done

  2. Use - will be given to test script to use

  3. Tear down - once test completed, remaing scripts will run

notes.question: Page vs Context vs Browser
notes.answer:
Browser -> This is the physical browser instance (Chromium, Firefox, or WebKit).

Context -> This is an isolated session within the browser.

Page -> This is a single Tab or window within a Browser Context.

notes.question: Self Healing Functionality development
notes.answer:
Can create a wrapper classes for each commands, like click, visibility, enable...

in try catch, if found webelement not there error, send DOM content and xpath to find the matching XPath through LLM.

// HealablePage.js
const { OpenAI } = require('openai'); // or your preferred LLM
const openai = new OpenAI({ apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY });

class HealablePage {
  constructor(page) {
    this.page = page;
  }

  async click(selector) {
    try {
      // Step 1: Normal attempt
      await this.page.click(selector, { timeout: 5000 });
    } catch (error) {
      console.warn(`Element ${selector} not found. Attempting self-healing...`);
      
      // Step 2: Get DOM and ask LLM
      const newSelector = await this._getHealedSelector(selector);
      
      if (newSelector) {
        console.log(`Healing successful! New selector: ${newSelector}`);
        await this.page.click(newSelector); // Step 3: Retry
      } else {
        throw error; // If LLM fails, throw original error
      }
    }
  }

  async _getHealedSelector(brokenSelector) {
    const dom = await this.page.content(); // Get full HTML
    
    const prompt = `
      The Playwright selector "${brokenSelector}" failed. 
      Based on the following HTML, find the correct new CSS or XPath selector for this element.
      Only return the selector string.
      HTML: ${dom.substring(0, 10000)} // Truncate to save tokens
    `;

    const response = await openai.chat.completions.create({
      model: "gpt-4-turbo",
      messages: [{ role: "user", content: prompt }],
    });

    return response.choices[0].message.content.trim();
  }
}

module.exports = { HealablePage };

notes.question: API Testing (SOAP and Rest)
notes.answer:
By Using Request fixture, can make the API Calls. For soap, change the Content-type in headers.

request - (Standalone fixture for API calls), Page.request -(Broswer cookies also can be added)

const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');

test('should fetch user details', async ({ request }) => {
  // Sending a GET request
  const response = await request.get('https://reqres.in/api/users/2');

  // Asserting the status code
  expect(response.status()).toBe(200);
  expect(response.ok()).toBeTruthy();

  // Parsing and asserting the JSON body
  const body = await response.json();
  expect(body.data.id).toBe(2);
});

Other types of API calls:

  • POST: await request.post(url, { data: { key: 'value' } }).

  • PUT/PATCH: await request.put(url, { data: { ... } }) for updating resources.

  • DELETE: await request.delete(url) to remove resources.

  • Query Params: Pass parameters using the params property: await request.get(url, { params: { id: 123 } })

Assetions:

  • expect(response).toBeOK(): Checks if the status is in the 200–299 range.

  • response.status(): Returns the numerical status code (e.g., 201 for "Created").

  • response.json(): Returns the JSON representation of the body for deep object assertions.

  • response.headers(): Returns an object with response headers.

To add certificates:

import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  use: {
    clientCertificates: [{
      origin: 'https://api.example.com', // Must match exactly
      certPath: './certs/client.pem',    // Path to PEM certificate
      keyPath: './certs/client.key',     // Path to private key
      passphrase: 'my-password',         // Optional: for encrypted keys
    }],
  },
});

Global Config for authentication:

You can simplify your tests by setting a baseURL and common headers (like Auth tokens) in your playwright.config.js file

module.exports = {
  use: {
    baseURL: 'https://api.example.com',
    extraHTTPHeaders: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${process.env.API_TOKEN}`,
      'Accept': 'application/json',
    },
  },
};

notes.question: Playwright vs Selenium
notes.answer:
Architecture:

PlayWright - Direct browser control via WebSocket (CDP/WebKit).

Selenium - Uses WebDriver protocol as an intermediary layer.

Speed:

PlayWright - Significantly faster due to lower latency and lack of extra HTTP calls.

Selenium - Generally slower due to protocol overhead.

Wait Handling:

PlayWright - Auto-waiting built-in for elements to be visible/clickable.

Selenium - Requires manual implementation of explicit or implicit waits.

API Testing:

PlayWright - In build liberary for API validation

Selenium - external API lib req

notes.question: Java String Methods
notes.answer:
String Str = "hello";

Fetch Data:

  1. S1.indexOf(S2) - > returns index of char, S1.indexOf(S2,{startindex}) and S1.lastIndexOf(S2)

  2. Str.charAt(0); -> returns char

  3. S1.length() -> returns length

  4. String.format(a,"Vas",i)) -> "hi %s, time %d"

Verify data:

  1. S1.contains(S2)

  2. S1.equals(S2),

  3. S1.equalsIgnoreCase(S2)

  4. S1.startsWith(S2) and S1.endsWith(S2) -> Boolean

  5. S1.isEmpty() -> Boolean

Manipulate data:

  1. S1.concat(S2);

  2. S1.replace('l', 'p')

  3. S1.replaceFirst("l","o")

  4. S1.replaceAll("\\d","X") for regex patern like \\d (numbers) and . for all (char or string)

  5. S1.Split("") -> returns an array. convert it to List by Arrays.asList()

  6. S1.substring(7, 12) -> get substring

  7. S1.toCharArray()

  8. S1.toLowerCase() and S1.toUpperCase()

  9. String.join(" ", "Orange", "Apple", "Mango");

  10. S1.trim()

notes.answer:

  • toString() -> to String

  • max(int a, int b)/min(int a, int b)/sum(int a, int b)

notes.question: How to enable traces?
notes.answer:
1. Global Level (Configuration File)

  2. Project Level

  3. Command Line

  4. Manual/Programmatic Level

test('manual trace', async ({ browser }) => {
  const context = await browser.newContext();
  
  // Start tracing
  await context.tracing.start({ screenshots: true, snapshots: true });

  const page = await context.newPage();
  await page.goto('https://example.com');
  await page.click('#submit');

  // Stop and save to a specific file
  await context.tracing.stop({ path: 'trace-results/manual-trace.zip' });
});

notes.question: Network Interception
notes.answer:
To have control over network calls. I can manipute the req,res and abort the calls

  1. Mocking (fulfil) -> fetch and overwrite the fake response

await page.route("https://rahulshettyacademy.com/api/ecom/order/get-orders-for-customer/*",
    async route => {
      const response = await page.request.fetch(route.request());
      let body = JSON.stringify(fakePayLoadOrders);
      route.fulfill(
        {
          response,
          body, 
        });
      //intercepting response -APi response-> { playwright fakeresponse}->browser->render data on front end
    });

  1. Modify (Continue) -> over write the req payload or url itself

await page.route("https://rahulshettyacademy.com/api/ecom/order/get-orders-details?id=*",
        route => route.continue({ url: 'https://rahulshettyacademy.com/api/ecom/order/get-orders-details?id=621661f884b053f6765465b6' }))

  1. Abort 

page.route('**/*.{jpg,png,jpeg}',route=> route.abort());

notes.question: Types of timeouts
notes.answer:
1. Test - overall execution timeout

  2. Expect - For webelement specific timeout

  3. Navigation - for broweser navigation

  4. action - click, check action level timeout

notes.question: Page Object Model (POM)
notes.answer:
To increase code reusability and easy maintanance we will keep UI functions here.

Under class, we will call constructor to initiate the Page objects, later in script we will create instance for this class to use it.

class LoginPage {
  constructor(page) {
    this.page = page;
    this.usernameInput = page.locator('#user-name');
    this.passwordInput = page.locator('#password');
    this.loginButton = page.locator('#login-button');
  }

  async login(user, pass) {
    await this.usernameInput.fill(user);
    await this.passwordInput.fill(pass);
    await this.loginButton.click();
  }
}
module.exports = { LoginPage };

notes.question: Playwright plugins
notes.answer: For custom reporting we have allure pluging

notes.question: How you will handle flakyness and network slowness
notes.answer:
Network Slowness:

  1. give action level time outs await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click({ timeout: 10000 });

  2. await page.goto('/dashboard', { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });

  3. API Interception -> await page.route('**/analytics.js', route => route.abort());

UI Slowness

  1. Web-First Assertions: await expect(page.locator('.status')).toHaveText('Loaded');

  2. playwright.config.js -> increase retries

Summary Strategy

  1. Mock slow dependencies.

  2. Retry tests at the config level.

  3. Auto-wait using Web-First assertions.

  4. Trace failures to debug the "why."

notes.question: Setup and teardown (Fixture vs POM vs Test)
Helper Util classes
notes.answer:
These are simple JS files,

it willl be having functions or classes , and later exported as whole

By calling the constructor we can intialise the values here.

We can have so many helper for xml parser, API supporter, DB validations, Excel utils

const {Test1,Test2} = require ('./Env_Var');

function getEnvConfig(){
    const env = process.env.Test_Env ;
    switch(env) {
        case 'Test1': return Test1;
        case 'Test2' : return Test2;
        default: return Test1;
    }
}

module.exports={getEnvConfig};

notes.question: Global Setup and Teardown (global authentication)
notes.answer:
Create js for both setup and teardown, and config in config file.

// global-setup.js
const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');

module.exports = async config => {
  console.log('🚀 Global Setup: Starting Database and Logging in...');
  
  // Example: Perform global login once
  const browser = await chromium.launch();
  const page = await browser.newPage();
  await page.goto('https://example.com');
  await page.fill('#user', 'admin');
  await page.fill('#pass', 'password');
  await page.click('#submit');
  
  // Save storage state to reuse in tests
  await page.context().storageState({ path: 'state.json' });
  await browser.close();
};
// global-teardown.js
module.exports = async config => {
  console.log('🏁 Global Teardown: Cleaning up Database and Closing Connections...');
  // Add your cleanup logic here
};
// playwright.config.js
module.exports = {
  globalSetup: require.resolve('./global-setup'),
  globalTeardown: require.resolve('./global-teardown'),
  use: {
    // Reuse the auth state saved in global setup
    storageState: 'state.json',
  },
};
